_comment: Golden contract for the Session Recall parser (Phase 1, Swift).
These are the CORRECT expected parse results for the fixtures under home/ -
NOT a snapshot of the Python prototype, which has two known bugs captured in
prototype_deltas below. A conforming parser MUST reproduce the `records` here.
Fields asserted per record: agent, cwd (unless cwd_recovery=ambiguous),
repo, session_id, resume_id, timestamp (ISO8601 UTC), title, ai_title.

records.title: Add pagination to the products API endpoint

records.fixture: claude/aaaaaaa2 (wrapper first user, list content, no aiTitle)
records.title: Refactor the auth middleware to use async/await
records.note: First user turn is a slash-command wrapper (<command-name>/init) and MUST be skipped for the title.

records.title: Fix the flaky login test
records.note: The malformed 2nd line MUST be skipped without crashing. No in-record cwd exists; the encoded dir name '-Users-alice-Repos-acme-web' is LOSSY (a literal '-' in a path segment is indistinguishable from an encoded '/'), so cwd is NOT reliably recoverable and MUST NOT be asserted. A correct parser prefers in-record cwd and marks this best-effort/unknown. This is the case that justifies the Phase 2 launch-time ledger.

records.title: Write integration tests for the billing webhook
records.note: Prompt arrives via event_msg/user_message (plain `message` string), NOT response_item/message. A correct parser MUST read both channels. The developer-role permissions block MUST be skipped.

records.fixture: codex/e2 (wrapper first user via response_item, real prompt after)
records.title: Investigate the memory leak in the worker pool
records.note: First user turn is the injected 'The following is the Codex agent history...' wrapper and MUST be skipped for the title.

records.title: (no prompt found)
records.note: Only wrapper/system/task events exist. Session MUST still be listed with title fallback. Timestamp is recoverable from the filename even if payloads lacked one.

prototype_deltas.expected: Write integration tests for the billing webhook
prototype_deltas.cause: The Python spike only reads response_item/message user turns; it never inspects event_msg/user_message. Real Codex logs use both channels, so the spike under-titles real sessions.
prototype_deltas.phase1_requirement: Swift parser reads BOTH event_msg/user_message and response_item/message.

prototype_deltas.expected: cwd unknown/best-effort (ambiguous); repo not reliably recoverable
prototype_deltas.cause: The spike decodes the encoded dir by replacing every '-' with '/', which mangles path segments containing a literal hyphen (acme-web -> acme/web). The encoding is lossy and cannot be inverted.
prototype_deltas.phase1_requirement: Swift parser ALWAYS prefers in-record cwd (and, in-app, the Phase 2 launch ledger). Encoded-dir decode is a last-ditch, explicitly-lossy fallback, never authoritative.
